marketFocus: Trump 'War Could Be Over Soon': US President told CBS the Iran conflict is 'very complete — they have no navy, no communications, no air force.' WTI collapsed from $119/bbl peak to $81/bbl post-settlement (2nd biggest daily move ever), US equities reversed to close green (SPX +0.84%), Dollar slammed, DXY beneath 99.00. G7 Energy Ministers meet today on coordinated SPR release (300-400mln bbls discussed).

China Trade Massive Beat (Bullish AUD/NZD): China Trade Balance Jan-Feb $213.6B (exp. $179.6B). Exports +19.2% YoY (exp. +7.1%), Imports +19.8% YoY (exp. +6.3%) — double-digit beats signal strong Chinese demand. APAC stocks rallied, Hang Seng and Shanghai Comp in the green. PBoC mid-point 6.8982 (prev. 6.9158) — CNY strengthening.

Japan GDP Final Q4 Confirms Recovery: GDP QoQ +0.3% (in-line), Annualized +1.3% (exp. +1.2%) — slight beat. However, Household Spending Jan -2.5% MoM (exp. +0.8%) — significant miss. German Trade Balance Jan €21.2B (exp. €15.2B) — strong beat. UK BRC Retail Sales Feb +0.7% YoY (exp. +2.4%) — miss. Australian Westpac Consumer Confidence Mar +1.2% (prev. -2.6%) — recovery.

USD.summary: Dollar was slammed after Trump's 'war could be over soon' comments sent DXY beneath 99.00, reversing the entire Monday haven surge. The geopolitical risk premium that had been the core USD bull case is now rapidly deflating. Asia session saw a mild DXY rebound but the trend is unclear — if Trump confirms ceasefire progress today, DXY could test 98.00. US CPI tomorrow (18:30 Frankfurt) is now the next major catalyst. Stagflation narrative remains but geopolitical haven bid is fading. No clear directional conviction today.
drivers: DXY slammed below 99.00 — Trump 'war over soon' killed haven premium
Mild Asia rebound but trend unclear — geopolitical risk premium deflating
US CPI tomorrow 18:30 Frankfurt — next major catalyst
Stagflation narrative intact but haven bid fading rapidly
Fed rate cut pricing: first 25bps not fully priced until December (36.4bps)

EUR.summary: EUR/USD clawed back early losses and reclaimed 1.1600 territory, but stalled — range 1.1507-1.1599. The brief recovery was entirely dollar-driven, not EUR strength. ECB hike pricing was reduced from 2 hikes to 1 hike by year-end after Trump's comments, but the structural bearish case remains: Eurozone is a net energy importer, LNG disruption persists, and German Trade Balance beat (€21.2B vs €15.2B exp) was the only positive. Newsflow is sparse today. EUR/USD faces resistance at 1.1600-1.1620. Bearish bias maintained.
drivers: EUR/USD stalled at 1.1600 — dollar-driven recovery, not EUR strength
ECB hike pricing reduced: 2 hikes → 1 hike by year-end post-Trump comments
German Trade Balance Jan €21.2B (exp. €15.2B) — beat, but not EUR positive
LNG disruption and energy importer status — structural bearish headwind
Sparse data calendar today — entirely geopolitics and dollar flow driven

GBP.summary: GBP steadily rebounded throughout Monday to return to 1.3400 territory, stalled below 1.3450 resistance. Price action mostly dictated by dollar moves — GBP benefited from the dollar selloff post-Trump comments. UK BRC Retail Sales Feb +0.7% YoY (exp. +2.4%) was a miss, but the dominant driver remains BoE easing priced out for 2026, keeping 2-year Gilt yields above 4.00%. UK Chancellor Reeves confirmed readiness to support coordinated IEA oil reserve release. GBP is aligned Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish — High Conviction setup confirmed.
drivers: GBP/USD rebounded to 1.3400, stalled below 1.3450 resistance
BoE easing priced out for 2026 — 2Y Gilt yields above 4.00%
UK BRC Retail Sales Feb +0.7% YoY (exp. +2.4%) — miss but not market-moving
UK Chancellor Reeves: ready to support IEA oil reserve release
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

JPY.summary: USD/JPY slipped below 158.00 after Trump's comments but lacks clear direction. Japan GDP Q4 Final confirmed recovery (+0.3% QoQ, +1.3% annualized) but Household Spending Jan -2.5% MoM (exp. +0.8%) was a significant miss — consumer weakness undermines BoJ's case for further hikes. Japan remains a 90%+ oil importer — even with oil pulling back from $119/bbl, the structural energy cost burden persists. BoJ March hike probability near zero. JPY is bearish but Trump 'war over' comments reduce the immediate pressure.
drivers: USD/JPY below 158.00 — lacks direction after Trump comments
Japan Household Spending Jan -2.5% MoM (exp. +0.8%) — significant miss
Japan GDP Q4 Final +0.3% QoQ confirmed — but consumer weakness limits BoJ
Japan 90%+ oil importer — structural energy burden persists
BoJ March hike probability near zero — policy divergence vs Fed

AUD.summary: China Trade Balance Jan-Feb: $213.6B (exp. $179.6B) — massive beat. Exports +19.2% YoY (exp. +7.1%), Imports +19.8% YoY (exp. +6.3%). This is unambiguously bullish for AUD as China is Australia's largest trading partner. ASX 200 rallied with gains led by miners, materials, tech and healthcare. Australian Westpac Consumer Confidence Mar +1.2% (prev. -2.6%) — recovery. RBA Deputy Governor Hauser confirmed 'genuine policy debate at board meeting with arguments on both sides.' AUD is Daily Bullish but Weekly Neutral — no High Conviction alignment, but strong intraday case.
drivers: China Exports +19.2% YoY (exp. +7.1%) — massive beat, direct AUD positive
China Imports +19.8% YoY (exp. +6.3%) — strong demand signal
ASX 200 rallied — miners, materials, tech outperformed
Westpac Consumer Confidence Mar +1.2% (prev. -2.6%) — recovery
RBA Deputy Hauser: genuine policy debate — not pre-committed to cuts

CAD.summary: WTI settled $94.77/bbl on Monday (still elevated despite pullback from $119/bbl peak), and Brent at $98.96/bbl. CAD remains the G10 petro-currency beneficiary — even with oil pulling back, the level is still significantly above pre-war prices. The Loonie saw its best performance overnight as oil prices surged, then pared back as oil pared. G7 Energy Ministers meet today — a coordinated SPR release could pressure oil further, which would be a mild CAD headwind. But the structural petro-currency advantage remains intact. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI $94.77/bbl settlement — still elevated, CAD petro-currency advantage intact
Loonie best overnight performer on oil surge — pared as oil pared
G7 Energy Ministers meet today — SPR release could pressure oil mildly
Saudi Aramco CEO: 180mln bbl disruption so far, no storage issues
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment

CHF.summary: CHF haven demand is fading as Trump's 'war could be over soon' comments reduce the geopolitical risk premium. Euro Stoxx 50 futures +1.3% for Tuesday open — risk-on recovery reduces CHF safe-haven bid. SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 remains intact. Swiss data calendar is empty today. CHF is Weekly Weak Bullish but Daily Neutral — no High Conviction alignment. The haven trade that drove CHF last week is unwinding as war-end narrative gains traction.
drivers: Haven demand fading — Trump 'war over soon' reduces geopolitical premium
Euro Stoxx 50 futures +1.3% — risk-on recovery reduces CHF safe-haven bid
SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 — limits CHF upside
Empty Swiss data calendar today — entirely sentiment driven
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

NZD.summary: China Trade massive beat (+19.2% exports, +19.8% imports) is directly bullish for NZD as China is New Zealand's largest trading partner. Antipodeans eased back slightly in Asia after yesterday's advances, with little reaction seen to the China data — but the fundamental case is clear. Risk-on recovery post-Trump comments also supports NZD as a high-beta currency. However, NZD is Weekly Weak Bearish vs Daily Bullish — this is a MISMATCH, so no High Conviction setup. Intraday long bias only.
drivers: China Exports +19.2% YoY (exp. +7.1%) — direct NZD positive
China Imports +19.8% YoY (exp. +6.3%) — strong demand signal
Risk-on recovery — NZD as high-beta currency benefits
Antipodeans eased back slightly in Asia — mild profit-taking
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = MISMATCH, intraday only

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes gapped lower after oil surged Monday but completely reversed and more as oil pulled back and Trump said the Iran war could be over soon. 10-year yield fell 3.6bps to 4.096%, 30-year fell 4.2bps to 4.714%. Inflationary fears soothed as oil pulled back from extremes. NY Fed SCE Feb: median 1Y inflation expectations softened to 3.0% (prev. 3.1%). Fed rate cut pricing: first 25bps not fully priced until December. Bonds bid as geopolitical risk premium deflates.
drivers: T-notes reversed higher — oil pullback soothed inflation fears
10Y yield -3.6bps to 4.096%, 30Y -4.2bps to 4.714%
NY Fed SCE Feb: 1Y inflation expectations 3.0% (prev. 3.1%) — slight softening
Fed rate cut: first 25bps not fully priced until December
Geopolitical risk premium deflating — war-end narrative supports bonds

bonds.name: German Bund 10Y
bonds.summary: Bund futures returned to above 127.00 level with geopolitics and oil volatility the main catalysts. German Trade Balance Jan €21.2B (exp. €15.2B) — strong beat. ECB hike pricing reduced from 2 hikes to 1 hike by year-end after Trump's war-end comments. EUR 10bln of issuances scheduled (Schatz and Bund auctions today and tomorrow). Bunds recovering as oil-driven inflation fears ease.
drivers: Bund futures back above 127.00 — oil pullback reduces inflation fears
German Trade Balance Jan €21.2B (exp. €15.2B) — strong beat
ECB hike pricing: 2 hikes → 1 hike by year-end post-Trump comments
EUR 10bln issuances (Schatz today, Bund tomorrow) — supply pressure
Oil-driven inflation fears easing — Bund recovery supported

bonds.name: JGB 10Y
bonds.summary: 10yr JGB futures held on to most of prior day's after-hour gains amid easing of oil-related inflationary pressure. Japan ordered to prepare release of reserves. Japan GDP Q4 Final confirmed +0.3% QoQ, but Household Spending Jan -2.5% MoM (exp. +0.8%) — consumer weakness limits BoJ hawkish case. JGBs recovering alongside global bond rally as geopolitical risk premium deflates.
drivers: JGB futures held gains — oil-related inflationary pressure easing
Japan ordered to prepare release of reserves — government response
Japan Household Spending Jan -2.5% MoM — miss limits BoJ hawkish case
Japan GDP Q4 Final +0.3% QoQ confirmed — recovery intact
Global bond rally as geopolitical risk premium deflates
